$comment: c16 shipped <time.h> — the struct tm layout (56B, byte-identical glibc/Darwin) + time/localtime_r on a real libc. struct tm is format-INVARIANT (flat, no variant), so one corpus runs on the linux + native-arm64 + macos-latest legs. exit 42 iff sizeof(struct tm)==56 AND tm_year sits at offset 20 (libc wrote the year there via localtime_r, the program reads it there). RED-ON-DISABLE: a short/misordered struct tm → sizeof!=56 or the year read != written → exit 1. Release arm runs the optimizer over the field-offset arithmetic + the libc calls.
